<current_datetime>2026-05-06T01:15:16.677Z</current_datetime>

I have this test that uses [ExpectedException]. How should I modernize it?

```csharp
[TestMethod]
[ExpectedException(typeof(ArgumentNullException))]
public void CreateUser_NullEmail_ThrowsException()
{
    var service = new UserService();
    service.CreateUser(null, "John");
}
```

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking how to modernize a C# test that uses the `[ExpectedException]` attribute. This is a straightforward knowledge question - I don't need to use any tools.

The modern approach is to use `Assert.ThrowsException<T>()` (MSTest) or `Assert.Throws<T>()` (xUnit/NUnit) instead of the `[ExpectedException]` attribute.
